lens.included_activities: Independent lower-middle-market manufacturers of surfactants, finishing agents, and formulated assistants sold repeatedly to external industrial and consumer-product customers.
lens.excluded_activities: Captive production, non-operating entities, commodity distribution without manufacturing, and businesses outside the stated EBITDA band.
lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Repeat B2B sales of specification-qualified chemical products, often supported by formulation, quality, regulatory, and technical-service work.

a.bridge: BLS chemical-manufacturing material describes common occupations at the broader NAICS 325 level, while the chemist profile establishes that chemical manufacturing employs 29% of chemists. I translate the broader occupation mix into a wage-weighted task estimate for 325613: documentation, planning, purchasing, sales support, formulation search, and quality-data interpretation are exposed, while operators, laboratory execution, maintenance, and material handling are less substitutable. The broader-sector bridge may overstate exposure if small surfactant plants are more production-heavy.
a.rationale: AI can reduce clerical, specification, regulatory-document, scheduling, technical-sales, and analytical workload, but physical batch processing, sampling, maintenance, and accountable release decisions remain substantial.
a.change_evidence: A payroll-weighted task inventory from 8-12 independent 325613 plants, mapped to O*NET tasks and current software use, would materially revise the estimate.
caveats: No public six-digit wage-weighted task study was found.
Current automation and plant scale vary materially.

rho.rationale: Within five years, assistive uses around documents, planning, formulation search, and customer service should be implementable, but validation, system integration, proprietary-data quality, cybersecurity, and safety/regulatory accountability slow deployment.
rho.change_evidence: Plant-level pilots measuring adoption, error rates, validation effort, and realized labor hours across ERP, laboratory, regulatory, and sales workflows would move this range.
caveats: Bounded judgment rather than an industry adoption measurement.
EPA ingredient and release requirements illustrate accountability constraints but do not measure AI implementation.

e.rationale: Most independent manufacturers in the specified EBITDA band sell repeat products externally and therefore fit, but captive units, single-customer tolling dependence, weak environmental records, excessive customer concentration, or businesses that are primarily distributors reduce eligibility.
e.change_evidence: A firm-by-firm census using Reference Solutions, PitchBook, EPA facility records, and customer concentration interviews for the 16 injected candidates would establish eligibility.
caveats: The injected firm count is an estimate based on a margin bridge.
The Census definition confirms activity scope but not independence or transferability.

s5.bridge: Federal Reserve Small Business Credit Survey chartbooks measure owner-age demographics across small businesses, not qualifying control transfers in 325613. I use older-owner prevalence as directional succession pressure, then discount for family/internal succession, closures, and deals outside five years. This can overstate sales because age does not imply a market transfer and understate strategic corporate divestitures.
s5.rationale: A mature small-business ownership base creates succession events, but specialized chemical assets, environmental diligence, customer qualification, and family/internal transfers reduce qualifying external control deals.
s5.change_evidence: PitchBook/CapIQ transaction screening plus interviews with specialty-chemical brokers and owners covering completed and abandoned 325613 control sales since 2018 would materially change the estimate.
caveats: Proxy is economy-wide and not industry-specific.
No denominator-based five-year transfer series was found.

q.rationale: Specification-qualified formulations and technical support provide some pricing discretion, so implemented savings can persist. Competitive bids, contract renewals, customer procurement, and raw-material-linked pricing still pass some benefit through over five years.
q.change_evidence: Customer contracts, renewal histories, contribution-margin bridges, price-cost lag analyses, and win/loss interviews from several target firms would directly test retained benefit.
caveats: No public contract sample quantifies pass-through.
Retention differs between proprietary formulations and commodity surfactants.

d5.rationale: Surfactants remain functional inputs to cleaning and diverse formulations. Stable end-use volumes and demand for safer ingredients support modest real growth, while formulation efficiency, concentration, cyclicality, and substitution constrain it.
d5.change_evidence: Five-year constant-price shipment forecasts by surfactant chemistry and end market, validated against Census product-level shipments and customer volume plans, would move the range.
caveats: EPA sources establish use and compliance demand, not market growth.
End-market and chemistry mix are heterogeneous.

o.rationale: Physical chemical conversion, quality release, safe handling, environmental compliance, and customer qualification continue to require an accountable manufacturer. Software can improve workflows but cannot supply the material itself.
o.change_evidence: Customer make-versus-buy surveys, capacity announcements, and evidence of direct digital/self-service substitution by major end markets would alter this estimate.
caveats: Operator need could shift toward larger suppliers through consolidation.
The estimate concerns quantity requiring a manufacturer, not employment retained.

narrative.executive_view: The opportunity is a recurring specialty-chemical manufacturing base with meaningful administrative and technical-work augmentation, but only a modest share of total labor is plausibly substitutable because production is physical and regulated.
narrative.ai_impact: AI is most relevant to specification and safety-document drafting, formulation and literature search, quality-data review, production and procurement planning, quoting, and technical-sales support. Batch operation, laboratory work, maintenance, material movement, and final accountable release stay human-led.
narrative.value_capture: Qualified products and technical support can make savings sticky, especially in proprietary niches. Commodity competition and procurement-led repricing are the main paths by which customers capture part of the gain.
narrative.firm_availability: The supplied LMM population is small. Many firms should fit the repeat external-sales lens, but owner willingness, environmental liabilities, customer concentration, and strategic ownership can sharply shrink actionable supply.
narrative.demand_durability: Surfactants remain necessary physical ingredients, and safer-chemistry requirements can support reformulation work. Real volumes are more likely stable to modestly growing than displaced by software, though end-market cycles and more concentrated products matter.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: The largest uncertainties are six-digit occupation mix, actual current automation, transfer incidence, environmental liabilities, and contract-level pass-through. Commodity-heavy targets may have lower retention and weaker differentiation than the central case.
narrative.principal_driver: Repeat specification-qualified product demand supports durable operator-required revenue and some savings retention.
narrative.principal_weakness: Physical production limits the implementable labor pool, and the estimated LMM universe is small.

sources.title: Chemical Manufacturing: NAICS 325
sources.supports: BLS overview identifies common occupations in chemical manufacturing, including 124,330 chemical equipment operators and tenders in the displayed industry material.

sources.title: Chemists and Materials Scientists
sources.supports: BLS reports 86,800 chemist jobs in 2024 and 29% in chemical manufacturing, supporting the technical-work proxy.

sources.title: Safer Choice Standard and Criteria
sources.supports: EPA states surfactants reduce surface tension and describes ingredient evaluation and manufacturing-release documentation, supporting product function and compliance-accountability claims.

sources.title: ASM Industry Definition for Soap and Cleaning Compound Manufacturing
sources.supports: Census lists NAICS 325613 Surface Active Agent Manufacturing within the relevant manufacturing rollup.

sources.title: 2024 Firms in Focus chartbooks on small business data
sources.supports: Federal Reserve chartbooks provide owner-demographic evidence, including an age-of-owner chartbook, used only as a broad succession proxy.

sources.title: Safer Choice Criteria for Surfactants
sources.supports: EPA describes surfactants in cleaning products and environmental fate and toxicity criteria, supporting persistent physical function and regulatory constraints.
